channel.title: Mroya + S
channel.description: WordPress Theme for Freelancers and Agencies

wp:category.wp:category_description: Exploring ideas, strategies, and early thinking that shape meaningful digital experiences.
Articles about visual systems, UI decisions, and the creative process behind polished interfaces.
Practical insights into code, performance, and building reliable, scalable web solutions.
A curated stream of ideas, trends, and references that spark creativity and fresh perspectives.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Who we are

Our website address is: http://theme.mroya.eu.

Comments

When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymized string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

Media

If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

Cookies

If you leave a comment on our site you may opt-in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.

If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select "Remember Me", your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

Embedded content from other websites

Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.

Who we share your data with

If you request a password reset, your IP address will be included in the reset email.

How long we retain your data

If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognize and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.

What rights you have over your data

If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.

Where your data is sent

Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.

item.title: About
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Refined
Digital Craft

We craft bold, minimal, and timeless digital experiences. Each project blends design, motion, and technology to tell stories that feel as good as they look — shaped by curiosity, precision, and a passion for detail.

[ About Us ]

Based in the Czech Republic

ESTD. 2016

[ Our Journey ]

It started with a simple idea to create digital experiences that feel both human and timeless. Over time, our work evolved through exploration, curiosity, and attention to detail, blending design, motion, and technology into one cohesive language. Each project has been a step forward, shaping our perspective and refining the way we bring ideas to life.

Our Super Powers

Prototyping and User Research

Product and Visual Design

Creative Direction

Brand Identity

Recognition

Awwwards

Site Of The Day

x10

Developer Award

x4

Honourable Mention

x6

The FWA

Site Of The Day

x5

Honorable mention

x3

CSSDA

Website Of The Day

x3

Innovation Design Award

x3

UI Design Award

x2

UX Design Award

x2

The Webby Awards

Nominee

x1

Creative Circle

Shortlists

x4

Our work has earned recognition and awards from leading industry experts, celebrating creativity, innovation, and meaningful impact across digital platforms.

[ Our Team ]

We’re a tight-knit crew of designers, developers, and strategists united by a passion for building things that matter.

Jason Miller

Co-Founder

Emily Harris

Co-Founder

Tyler Brooks

Designer

Brandon Carter

Developer

Lauren Mitchell

Developer

[ Our Clients ]

Let’s
Connect

Whether you’re starting something new or improving an existing website, we’d be happy to hear from you. Share a few details about your project, and we’ll get back to you with thoughtful feedback and next steps.

Send a Message

item.title: Contact
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Contact
Our Studio

Whether you’re launching a brand, refining your product, or improving your online presence, we’re here to collaborate. Tell us what you need, and we’ll handle the strategy.

Contacts

Phone: +44 209 555 014

Email: info@mroya.com

Address

2118 Thornridge Cir. Syracuse,
Connecticut 35624

Working Hours

Mon–Sat: 9am to 5pm

Sunday – Closed

Ask a Question

[contact-form-7 id="2c93d4f" title="Contact form 1"]

item.title: Home
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Mroya
+ Studio...

We design and build modern websites with a strong focus on clarity and usability. Covering everything from visual design to frontend implementation, we turn ideas into digital experiences that feel intuitive and purposeful.

Scroll Down

Based in the Czech Republic

Local Time

[ Our mission ]We aim to give creators a flexible foundation that helps them present their work with clarity, confidence, and a distinct visual voice.

  • Brand Strategy
  • Visual Identity
  • UI/UX Design
  • Web Development
  • Front-End Solutions
  • Content Production
  • Design Systems
  • Logo Design
  • Landing Pages
  • Motion Graphics
  • Art Direction
  • Digital Projects

[ Portfolio ]

# Recent Projects

Sorry, but nothing was found.

See all Projects

[ Services ]

# How can we help

01

Design

We design intuitive, engaging interfaces where usability meets aesthetics. Clean, functional, and seamless experiences that inspire confidence and delight in every interaction.

#Research

#Wireframing

#Design_System

02

Development

We build fast, responsive, and reliable websites using modern technologies. With clean code, smart structure, and attention to detail, every element works in perfect harmony.

#Wordpress

#Webflow

#Framer

#Web3

03

The full package

Looking for an end-to-end solution? Our full package merges design and development into a seamless process, resulting in a website that’s fully tailored to your brand and audience.

#Landing_pages

#E-commerce

#Corporate

Testimonials

The studio guided us through the redesign with clarity and confidence. The final website strengthened our brand and noticeably improved user engagement.

Olivia Bennett

Horizon Studio

Communication was smooth from start to finish. They quickly understood our goals and turned them into a clean, modern website.

Daniel Foster

Apex Digital

Their attention to detail truly stood out. The team delivered a polished, high-performance site that exceeded our expectations.

Maya Rodriguez

Brightline Agency

We appreciated their creative approach and technical skill. The result feels authentic to our brand and was delivered right on time.

Lucas Turner

Nova Works

The team injected new energy into our brand with a bold and purposeful design. Collaboration was easy and productive throughout.

Chloe Adams

Skyline Creative

Let’s
Connect

Whether you’re starting something new or improving an existing website, we’d be happy to hear from you. Share a few details about your project, and we’ll get back to you with thoughtful feedback and next steps.

Send a Message

item.title: How You Can Improve Your Programming Skills
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Web design trends change constantly, but purpose-driven design remains timeless. Instead of following styles blindly, designers should ask what problem a layout is solving and how it supports the content. Purpose gives design its direction.

Typography, color, and spacing are not merely aesthetic choices — they influence mood, readability, and trust. When these elements work together, they create a coherent visual language that feels intentional and confident.

Collaboration with developers early in the process often leads to better results. Understanding technical limitations helps designers make smarter decisions and avoid unnecessary compromises later.

In the end, strong web design is measured by clarity and impact, not complexity. When purpose guides every choice, the result is an experience that feels both refined and meaningful.

item.title: Create beautiful numbered lists with CSS Counters
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Modern web design is no longer about decoration alone — it’s about creating interfaces that feel natural, intuitive, and calm. Users expect websites to respond instantly, guide them clearly, and avoid unnecessary friction. A successful interface quietly supports the user instead of demanding attention.

One of the most important aspects of effective UI design is structure. Clear spacing, consistent typography, and predictable layouts help users understand content without thinking about it. When hierarchy is done well, the interface almost disappears, allowing the message to come forward.

Performance First

Visual design and performance should never be treated as separate disciplines. Heavy animations, oversized images, or complex layouts can quickly undermine even the most beautiful interface. Optimizing assets and choosing the right interactions ensures that design remains smooth across all devices.

Another key factor is responsiveness. Interfaces must adapt gracefully to different screen sizes, input methods, and user contexts. Designing with flexibility in mind from the start saves time and leads to more resilient, future-proof solutions.

Ultimately, effortless interfaces are the result of many small, thoughtful decisions. When design and development work together, the end product feels simple — even though a great deal of care went into creating it.

item.title: Introduction to CSS Custom Properties
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Front-end development sits at the intersection of design and technology. It translates visual ideas into living, interactive experiences that users can touch and explore. A well-built front end respects both aesthetic intent and technical constraints.

Clean, readable code plays a major role in long-term project success. When components are structured clearly and logic is easy to follow, teams can iterate faster and reduce errors. Maintainability is just as important as visual output.

Good code is not only about making things work — it’s about creating systems that are clear, predictable, and easy to understand long after they are written. Code should support collaboration, reduce friction, and respect the people who will maintain and extend it in the future.

Anonymous

Performance is another essential consideration. Fast loading times, smooth transitions, and efficient rendering all contribute to a better user experience. Users may not notice optimization directly, but they feel it in every interaction.

By focusing on clarity, performance, and collaboration, front-end development becomes more than implementation. It becomes a craft that supports both designers and users alike.

item.title: Code Principles Every Programmer Should Follow
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Web design trends change constantly, but purpose-driven design remains timeless. Instead of following styles blindly, designers should ask what problem a layout is solving and how it supports the content. Purpose gives design its direction.

Typography, color, and spacing are not merely aesthetic choices — they influence mood, readability, and trust. When these elements work together, they create a coherent visual language that feels intentional and confident.

Collaboration with developers early in the process often leads to better results. Understanding technical limitations helps designers make smarter decisions and avoid unnecessary compromises later.

In the end, strong web design is measured by clarity and impact, not complexity. When purpose guides every choice, the result is an experience that feels both refined and meaningful.

item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Modern web design is no longer about decoration alone — it’s about creating interfaces that feel natural, intuitive, and calm. Users expect websites to respond instantly, guide them clearly, and avoid unnecessary friction. A successful interface quietly supports the user instead of demanding attention.

One of the most important aspects of effective UI design is structure. Clear spacing, consistent typography, and predictable layouts help users understand content without thinking about it. When hierarchy is done well, the interface almost disappears, allowing the message to come forward.

Performance First

Visual design and performance should never be treated as separate disciplines. Heavy animations, oversized images, or complex layouts can quickly undermine even the most beautiful interface. Optimizing assets and choosing the right interactions ensures that design remains smooth across all devices.

Another key factor is responsiveness. Interfaces must adapt gracefully to different screen sizes, input methods, and user contexts. Designing with flexibility in mind from the start saves time and leads to more resilient, future-proof solutions.

Ultimately, effortless interfaces are the result of many small, thoughtful decisions. When design and development work together, the end product feels simple — even though a great deal of care went into creating it.

Clean, readable code plays a major role in long-term project success. When components are structured clearly and logic is easy to follow, teams can iterate faster and reduce errors. Maintainability is just as important as visual output.

Good code is not only about making things work — it’s about creating systems that are clear, predictable, and easy to understand long after they are written. Code should support collaboration, reduce friction, and respect the people who will maintain and extend it in the future.

Performance is another essential consideration. Fast loading times, smooth transitions, and efficient rendering all contribute to a better user experience. Users may not notice optimization directly, but they feel it in every interaction.

By focusing on clarity, performance, and collaboration, front-end development becomes more than implementation. It becomes a craft that supports both designers and users alike.

item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Web design trends change constantly, but purpose-driven design remains timeless. Instead of following styles blindly, designers should ask what problem a layout is solving and how it supports the content. Purpose gives design its direction.

Typography, color, and spacing are not merely aesthetic choices — they influence mood, readability, and trust. When these elements work together, they create a coherent visual language that feels intentional and confident.

Collaboration with developers early in the process often leads to better results. Understanding technical limitations helps designers make smarter decisions and avoid unnecessary compromises later.

In the end, strong web design is measured by clarity and impact, not complexity. When purpose guides every choice, the result is an experience that feels both refined and meaningful.

One of the most important aspects of effective UI design is structure. Clear spacing, consistent typography, and predictable layouts help users understand content without thinking about it. When hierarchy is done well, the interface almost disappears, allowing the message to come forward.

Visual design and performance should never be treated as separate disciplines. Heavy animations, oversized images, or complex layouts can quickly undermine even the most beautiful interface. Another key factor is responsiveness. Interfaces must adapt gracefully to different screen sizes, input methods, and user contexts. Designing with flexibility in mind from the start saves time and leads to more resilient, future-proof solutions.

Ultimately, effortless interfaces are the result of many small, thoughtful decisions. When design and development work together, the end product feels simple — even though a great deal of care went into creating it.

In the end, strong web design is measured by clarity and impact, not complexity. When purpose guides every choice, the result is an experience that feels both refined and meaningful.

Client

Mroya Studio

Services

Branding, Design

Year

2025

item.title: What’s the next UI design trend?
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Modern web design is no longer about decoration alone — it’s about creating interfaces that feel natural, intuitive, and calm. Users expect websites to respond instantly, guide them clearly, and avoid unnecessary friction. A successful interface quietly supports the user instead of demanding attention.

One of the most important aspects of effective UI design is structure. Clear spacing, consistent typography, and predictable layouts help users understand content without thinking about it. When hierarchy is done well, the interface almost disappears, allowing the message to come forward.

Performance First

Visual design and performance should never be treated as separate disciplines. Heavy animations, oversized images, or complex layouts can quickly undermine even the most beautiful interface. Optimizing assets and choosing the right interactions ensures that design remains smooth across all devices.

Another key factor is responsiveness. Interfaces must adapt gracefully to different screen sizes, input methods, and user contexts. Designing with flexibility in mind from the start saves time and leads to more resilient, future-proof solutions.

Ultimately, effortless interfaces are the result of many small, thoughtful decisions. When design and development work together, the end product feels simple — even though a great deal of care went into creating it.

item.title: A Beginner’s Guide to Sass
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Front-end development sits at the intersection of design and technology. It translates visual ideas into living, interactive experiences that users can touch and explore. A well-built front end respects both aesthetic intent and technical constraints.

Clean, readable code plays a major role in long-term project success. When components are structured clearly and logic is easy to follow, teams can iterate faster and reduce errors. Maintainability is just as important as visual output.

Good code is not only about making things work — it’s about creating systems that are clear, predictable, and easy to understand long after they are written. Code should support collaboration, reduce friction, and respect the people who will maintain and extend it in the future.

Anonymous

Performance is another essential consideration. Fast loading times, smooth transitions, and efficient rendering all contribute to a better user experience. Users may not notice optimization directly, but they feel it in every interaction.

By focusing on clarity, performance, and collaboration, front-end development becomes more than implementation. It becomes a craft that supports both designers and users alike.

item.title: Create Stylish Links with CSS
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Web design trends change constantly, but purpose-driven design remains timeless. Instead of following styles blindly, designers should ask what problem a layout is solving and how it supports the content. Purpose gives design its direction.

Typography, color, and spacing are not merely aesthetic choices — they influence mood, readability, and trust. When these elements work together, they create a coherent visual language that feels intentional and confident.

Collaboration with developers early in the process often leads to better results. Understanding technical limitations helps designers make smarter decisions and avoid unnecessary compromises later.

In the end, strong web design is measured by clarity and impact, not complexity. When purpose guides every choice, the result is an experience that feels both refined and meaningful.

item.title: The Evolution of UX Process Methodology
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Modern web design is no longer about decoration alone — it’s about creating interfaces that feel natural, intuitive, and calm. Users expect websites to respond instantly, guide them clearly, and avoid unnecessary friction. A successful interface quietly supports the user instead of demanding attention.

One of the most important aspects of effective UI design is structure. Clear spacing, consistent typography, and predictable layouts help users understand content without thinking about it. When hierarchy is done well, the interface almost disappears, allowing the message to come forward.

Performance First

Visual design and performance should never be treated as separate disciplines. Heavy animations, oversized images, or complex layouts can quickly undermine even the most beautiful interface. Optimizing assets and choosing the right interactions ensures that design remains smooth across all devices.

Another key factor is responsiveness. Interfaces must adapt gracefully to different screen sizes, input methods, and user contexts. Designing with flexibility in mind from the start saves time and leads to more resilient, future-proof solutions.

Ultimately, effortless interfaces are the result of many small, thoughtful decisions. When design and development work together, the end product feels simple — even though a great deal of care went into creating it.

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: Clear writing and strong insights throughout. The examples and explanations made complex ideas easy to understand without oversimplifying them. This makes the article accessible to both beginners and experienced professionals.

This post highlights an important mindset that’s often overlooked — building for maintainability and future growth, not just immediate results. It’s a reminder that good decisions today save time and effort later on.
Very engaging read. I appreciate how the author focuses on both the creative and technical sides of web work in a balanced way. That balance is something many teams continue to strive for.

item.title: My Secret Weapon for Design Inspiration
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Front-end development sits at the intersection of design and technology. It translates visual ideas into living, interactive experiences that users can touch and explore. A well-built front end respects both aesthetic intent and technical constraints.

Clean, readable code plays a major role in long-term project success. When components are structured clearly and logic is easy to follow, teams can iterate faster and reduce errors. Maintainability is just as important as visual output.

Good code is not only about making things work — it’s about creating systems that are clear, predictable, and easy to understand long after they are written. Code should support collaboration, reduce friction, and respect the people who will maintain and extend it in the future.

Anonymous

Performance is another essential consideration. Fast loading times, smooth transitions, and efficient rendering all contribute to a better user experience. Users may not notice optimization directly, but they feel it in every interaction.

By focusing on clarity, performance, and collaboration, front-end development becomes more than implementation. It becomes a craft that supports both designers and users alike.

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: The article does a great job of explaining why simplicity is often the result of many small, intentional choices rather than minimal effort. That perspective is especially valuable for teams trying to improve their workflow over time.

I found the section about collaboration between designers and developers especially relevant. It reflects the challenges and benefits I’ve seen in my own projects. Strong communication really does make a noticeable difference in the final outcome.

item.title: Debug CSS Grid with Firefox DevTools
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
Web design trends change constantly, but purpose-driven design remains timeless. Instead of following styles blindly, designers should ask what problem a layout is solving and how it supports the content. Purpose gives design its direction.

Typography, color, and spacing are not merely aesthetic choices — they influence mood, readability, and trust. When these elements work together, they create a coherent visual language that feels intentional and confident.

Collaboration with developers early in the process often leads to better results. Understanding technical limitations helps designers make smarter decisions and avoid unnecessary compromises later.

In the end, strong web design is measured by clarity and impact, not complexity. When purpose guides every choice, the result is an experience that feels both refined and meaningful.

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: Really enjoyed reading this article. The way design decisions were connected with performance and usability made the topic feel very practical and grounded in real experience. It’s the kind of insight that’s immediately useful in everyday project work.

This was a thoughtful and well-structured piece. It’s refreshing to see an emphasis on clarity and long-term value instead of chasing short-lived trends. Articles like this encourage a more sustainable approach to design and development.

item.title: Contact form 1
item.dc:creator: Uladzimir Kulesh
item.content:encoded:
[text* your-name autocomplete:name id:your-name placeholder "Your name"]

[email* your-email autocomplete:email id:your-email placeholder "Your e-mail"]

[tel* your-phone autocomplete:tel id:your-phone placeholder "Your phone"]

[text* your-subject id:your-subject placeholder "Subject"]

[textarea your-message id:your-message placeholder "Your message"]

[submit class:wp-element-button "Send message"] 1 [_site_title] "[your-subject]" [_site_title] [_site_admin_email] From: [your-name] [your-email] Subject: [your-subject] Message Body: [your-message] -- This is a notification that a contact form was submitted on your website ([_site_title] [_site_url]). Reply-To: [your-email] 1 1 [_site_title] "[your-subject]" [_site_title] [your-email] Message Body: [your-message] -- This email is a receipt for your contact form submission on our website ([_site_title] [_site_url]) in which your email address was used. If that was not you, please ignore this message. Reply-To: [_site_admin_email] 1 1 Thank you for your message. It has been sent. There was an error trying to send your message. Please try again later. One or more fields have an error. Please check and try again. There was an error trying to send your message. Please try again later. You must accept the terms and conditions before sending your message. Please fill out this field. This field has a too long input. This field has a too short input. There was an unknown error uploading the file. You are not allowed to upload files of this type. The uploaded file is too large. There was an error uploading the file. Please enter a date in YYYY-MM-DD format. This field has a too early date. This field has a too late date. Please enter a number. This field has a too small number. This field has a too large number. The answer to the quiz is incorrect. Please enter an email address. Please enter a URL. Please enter a telephone number.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value:
[text* your-name autocomplete:name id:your-name placeholder "Your name"]

[email* your-email autocomplete:email id:your-email placeholder "Your e-mail"]

[tel* your-phone autocomplete:tel id:your-phone placeholder "Your phone"]

[text* your-subject id:your-subject placeholder "Subject"]

[textarea your-message id:your-message placeholder "Your message"]

[submit class:wp-element-button "Send message"]

a:9:{s:6:"active";b:0;s:7:"subject";s:30:"[_site_title] "[your-subject]"";s:6:"sender";s:40:"[_site_title] <wordpress@theme.mroya.eu>";s:9:"recipient";s:12:"[your-email]";s:4:"body";s:220:"Message Body: [your-message]
-- This email is a receipt for your contact form submission on our website ([_site_title] [_site_url]) in which your email address was used. If that was not you, please ignore this message.";s:18:"additional_headers";s:29:"Reply-To: [_site_admin_email]";s:11:"attachments";s:0:"";s:8:"use_html";b:1;s:13:"exclude_blank";b:1;}

a:22:{s:12:"mail_sent_ok";s:45:"Thank you for your message. It has been sent.";s:12:"mail_sent_ng";s:71:"There was an error trying to send your message. Please try again later.";s:16:"validation_error";s:61:"One or more fields have an error. Please check and try again.";s:4:"spam";s:71:"There was an error trying to send your message. Please try again later.";s:12:"accept_terms";s:69:"You must accept the terms and conditions before sending your message.";s:16:"invalid_required";s:27:"Please fill out this field.";s:16:"invalid_too_long";s:32:"This field has a too long input.";s:17:"invalid_too_short";s:33:"This field has a too short input.";s:13:"upload_failed";s:46:"There was an unknown error uploading the file.";s:24:"upload_file_type_invalid";s:49:"You are not allowed to upload files of this type.";s:21:"upload_file_too_large";s:31:"The uploaded file is too large.";s:23:"upload_failed_php_error";s:38:"There was an error uploading the file.";s:12:"invalid_date";s:41:"Please enter a date in YYYY-MM-DD format.";s:14:"date_too_early";s:32:"This field has a too early date.";s:13:"date_too_late";s:31:"This field has a too late date.";s:14:"invalid_number";s:22:"Please enter a number.";s:16:"number_too_small";s:34:"This field has a too small number.";s:16:"number_too_large";s:34:"This field has a too large number.";s:23:"quiz_answer_not_correct";s:36:"The answer to the quiz is incorrect.";s:13:"invalid_email";s:30:"Please enter an email address.";s:11:"invalid_url";s:19:"Please enter a URL.";s:11:"invalid_tel";s:32:"Please enter a telephone number.";}
